N1.title: 97.5% of real workers scored cleaner than even the lowest-scoring paid fabricator
N1.homepage_teaser: We paid FLWs bonuses to defeat our fraud detection — they couldn't. 97.5% scored cleaner than the lowest fabricator.

N2.title: Microplanning produced a 3.5× increase in visits-per-child — 1.4 vs 0.4
N2.homepage_teaser: In CHC, microplans tripled visits-per-child from 0.4 to 1.4 and pushed coverage from 84% to 94%.

N3.title: We measure verified-visit cost — and CHC fell 22% as it scaled
N3.homepage_teaser: In CHC, cost per verified visit fell 22% — Nigeria $1.30 with ORS, outside Nigeria $0.78 without.

N4.title: We invented the Trial Run because pre-vetting LLOs didn't predict performance
N4.homepage_teaser: We invented the Trial Run because pre-vetting LLOs didn't predict performance — 65% conversion through it.

N5.title: Knowledge ≠ competence — and we have ~400 FLWs of evidence
N5.homepage_teaser: Knowledge tests didn't produce competent FLWs — we layered an AI coach. 88% score above 70% on first observed visits.

N6.title: Caregivers' attitudes were already correct — we moved knowledge and behavior, not beliefs
N6.homepage_teaser: Caregivers' ECD attitudes were already correct — we moved knowledge +33% and observed teaching behavior +21%.

N7.title: LLOs invented vaccine-hesitancy outreach with religious leaders before we asked

N8.title: Both LLOs in CAR dropped out — we now flag fragile contexts as needing in-country presence

N9.title: 65% LLO conversion through Trial Run — we publish what we drop

N10.title: Guardrails are not hard stops — KMC currently failing one (50% vs 70%)

N11.title: KMC reduces neonatal mortality up to 40% — global coverage below 5%

N13.title: Cost asymmetry by commodity bundle — Nigeria $1.30 with ORS, outside $0.78 without

N14.title: AI coach handled 500 of 511 FLW queries — 97.8% — without human escalation

N15.title: Campaign work in Nigeria isn't a disruption of routine care — it is the routine care

N18.title: We chose verified-visit cost reporting because cost-per-FLW-trained hides the lever that matters

N20.title: Across 4,005 KMC cases, average enrollment 12.8 days — under target. Still missing 70%-within-3-days at 50%

N22.title: Connect verifies through four independent layers — biometric, GPS, photo, algorithmic

nuggets_not_yet_landed: N16 (overlaps with N9, framed for funder lens)
N19 (KMC FLW equipment — tangible, less argument)

top_3_by_combined_score_and_depth_potent.verbatim: We paid experienced FLWs to fake 100 visit records each across three rounds and offered bonuses to the top 25% most convincing fabricators. Out of 33 adversarial fakers, 97.5% of real workers scored cleaner than even the lowest-scoring fabricator — using just three data points (age, gender, MUAC). AUC 0.91 ± 0.09.

Our Malawi ECD pilot (76 FLWs, 9,510+ visits, 50-caregiver baseline/endline) found nearly all caregivers already held positive ECD attitudes at baseline. The intervention didn't need to convince them — it needed to close the gap between belief and practice. Knowledge of rapid-development timing rose +33%; observed teaching behavior +21%; encouragement of autonomy +21% but still only 47% at endline. So we doubled the dosage from 3 visits to 8–10 across 12 months.

Funders routinely ask whether paid campaign work crowds out volunteer health work. We commissioned 24 in-depth interviews in Gombe and Sokoto (CHC, 2025). Campaigns are not a disruption of Nigeria's CHV system — they are the system. CHVs routinely run campaigns, and other volunteers cover routine work during them. In Gombe, FLWs maintained volunteer hours by shifting schedules within Connect's 7am–7pm window.
